# Upstream Pleroma root-history audit ledger

This ledger tracks upstream Pleroma `develop` commits in root-history order, starting from the first upstream commit in 2017. It exists so Unfathomably can review each upstream change once and avoid repeatedly rechecking the same commits.

Audit policy: keep three strict queues. This is intentionally not a 2026-only
review. The current-tree queue continues from the active Rebased develop
merge-base so newer upstream Pleroma work does not get rechecked or skipped.
The reopened historical queue starts at the earliest fetched Rebased lineage
lower bound in 2021 and must be confirmed one commit at a time instead of
trusted only because it appears in branch ancestry. The deep historical
compatibility queue covers rows before that lower bound. Those rows are common
upstream ancestry for merge-base purposes, but common ancestry is not the same
thing as proof that fork-era behavior still matches upstream after years of
local changes. If a newly fetched Rebased branch or tag exposes an older
merge-base, move the reopened historical cursor back to that row and
reclassify the newly exposed range before advancing the current-tree cursor.

Current upstream snapshot: `pleroma-upstream/develop` after the most recent fetch.
Total upstream commits in snapshot: 17947
Latest upstream row: 17947 (`b0ae45194`, 2026-06-29, `Merge pull request 'Allow attaching emojis to user lists' (#7929) from mkljczk/pleroma:list-emoji into develop`)

## Strict audit state

Current local merge-base: row 15431 (`ca1b18ba2`, 2023-11-15, `Merge branch 'federation_status-access' into 'develop'`)
Current-tree strict cursor: `17000`
Reopened historical strict cursor: `15431`
Reopened historical queue: closed through row `15431` on 2026-07-04 after reopening the older Rebased lineage window and checking direct ancestry plus every non-ancestor exception.
Reopened historical rows remaining in queue: `0`
Deep historical compatibility cursor: `5250`
Deep historical compatibility queue: rows `1` through `13255` are reopened on 2026-07-04 for source-level confirmation where upstream behavior may have been changed, reverted, or made obsolete by Rebased and Unfathomably work.
Deep historical compatibility rows remaining in queue: `8005`
Historical baseline notes:

- Earliest fetched Rebased branch merge-base: row `13255` (`b221d77a6`, 2021-03-02, `Merge branch 'release/2.3.0' into 'stable'`). This gives us the conservative lower bound for the reopened historical audit.
- Soapbox build lineage merge-base: row `13366` (`f8cef7041`, 2021-04-01, `Merge branch 'chore/CHANGELOG' into 'develop'`).
- Rebased groups branch merge-base: row `13824` (`b11dbbf40`, 2021-08-17, `UserController: fall back to frontend when user isn't found`). Group and Threadiverse-related commits after this point need special attention.
- Active Rebased develop merge-base: row `15431` (`ca1b18ba2`, 2023-11-15, `Merge branch 'federation_status-access' into 'develop'`). The current-tree strict queue starts after this row.
- Rebased main/stable lineage merge-base: row `16195` (`7566b4a34`, 2024-05-22, `Merge branch 'release-2.6.3' into 'stable'`). This is later than the active develop merge-base and remains covered by the current-tree queue.

Fetched Rebased branch merge-base evidence:

| Ref family | Earliest upstream row | Date | Subject | Why it matters |
| --- | ---: | --- | --- | --- |
| `origin/pleroma-ci` | `13255` | 2021-03-02 | Merge branch `release/2.3.0` into `stable` | Earliest fetched Rebased-side upstream ancestor. This is the current hard lower bound for the reopened historical queue. |
| `origin/build-soapbox`, `origin/fix-test-timeout` | `13366` | 2021-04-01 | Merge branch `chore/CHANGELOG` into `develop` | Soapbox build lineage starts shortly after the earliest lower bound. |
| Rebased tag `soapbox-v1.0.0` | `13453` | 2021-05-05 | Notice compatibility routes | Earliest fetched Rebased-side tag after refreshing remote tag evidence. Tags do not move the lower bound earlier than `origin/pleroma-ci`. |
| Rebased tags `soapbox-v1.1.0`, `soapbox-v1.1.1` | `13471` | 2021-05-16 | Runtime config gitignore | Additional Soapbox-era tag evidence. |
| `origin/groups` | `13824` | 2021-08-17 | UserController frontend fallback | Group work appears after this point, so Threadiverse compatibility audits must not start only from the 2023 active develop base. |
| `origin/pleroma-events` | `14784` | 2022-11-20 | Merge branch `akoma/deactivated-users` into `develop` | Event-era Rebased work is still before the active 2023 develop base. |
| Rebased tag `v3.0.0` | `14784` | 2022-11-20 | Merge branch `akoma/deactivated-users` into `develop` | Rebased v3-era tag evidence lines up with the event branch merge-base. |
| `origin/pleroma`, `origin/more-fixes` | `15281` | 2023-09-03 | Attachment attribution merge | Late pre-active-develop lineage remains part of the historical audit. |
| `origin/develop`, `origin/csp-blob` | `15431` | 2023-11-15 | Federation status access merge | Active Rebased develop base. Current-tree strict review starts after this row. |
| `origin/main`, `origin/hackney-1.23` | `16195` | 2024-05-22 | Release 2.6.3 merge | Newer Rebased main/stable work does not let us skip the older develop split range. |

If additional archived Rebased refs are recovered with an upstream merge-base
older than row `13255`, rows before `13256` must be reopened and classified in
the same strict one-commit-at-a-time style. Until then, rows before `13256`
stay visible as base history, not as a hidden assumption.

Scope note:

- This is not a 2026-only review. The current strict post-merge-base queue begins at row `15432`, immediately after the active 2023-11-15 merge-base, and runs through the latest fetched upstream row.
- Rows `13256` through `15431` are the older Rebased lineage window. That window is reopened for one-by-one confirmation; existing `present`, `superseded`, and `not-applicable` entries there are starting hypotheses until the reopened historical cursor passes them.
- Historical strict confirmation through row `15431`: rows `13256` through `15431` were reopened on 2026-07-04. Direct-ancestor rows are confirmed inherited; non-ancestor exceptions were checked against current source and classified individually. The only useful runtime source item still intentionally deferred in this window is the Vips media-preview replacement, because it changes deployment prerequisites.
- Rows after `15431` must also be reviewed one at a time in this ledger before the current-tree strict cursor moves past them. Batch notes are only hints.
- Rows before `13256` are not deleted or ignored. They remain visible as
  `base-history` because the currently fetched Rebased lineage does not expose
  an older merge-base, but they are now also part of the deep historical
  compatibility queue. If we recover an older Rebased reference, that older
  range becomes part of the reopened historical queue rather than staying
  only in the compatibility queue.
Next historical strict row to review: none; the reopened Rebased lineage window through row `15431` is closed.
Next deep historical compatibility row to review: `5251` (`56fd7dbdd`, `2019-05-25`, `remove @websub and @ostatus module-level constants`)
Next current-tree strict row to review: `17001` (`3b74d1314`, 2025-02-22, `Do not call LanguageDetector when not language is provided`)
Current-tree strict rows remaining in snapshot: `947`

This block covers an OpenBSD install-doc correction, ActivityPub C2S Update authorization hardening, Mastodon-compatible followed hashtags, and backup archive link handling. Runtime backports from this block were applied where useful; CI, release, changelog, and merge-only rows were classified without source changes.

| 16952 | `b51f5a84e` | 2024-10-15 | Verify a local Update sent through AP C2S so users can only update their own objects | Backported and modernized. C2S `Update` validation now receives request metadata, checks local object access through `Object.authorize_access/2`, and returns clean 400 errors for rejected changesets. |

## Deep historical compatibility rows 1201-1300

These rows cover early 2018 upstream work around gopher output, upload
limits, Mastodon API compatibility, XML/WebFinger hardening, search, lists,
MRF, unrepeat/unannounce federation, and old frontend packaging. They are
common-history era commits, but each row below was checked against the current
Unfathomably source shape so old behavior is not assumed blindly.

| Row | Commit | Date | Subject | Disposition |

## Deep historical compatibility rows 1301-1400

These rows cover early proxy/config generation cleanup, Qvitter-era API
compatibility, MRF attachment and media handling, unreblog federation fixes,
note-count accounting, profile bio normalization, public timeline restrictions,
NodeInfo, idempotency keys, and custom test database parameters.

## Deep historical compatibility rows 1401-1500

These rows include early websocket/stream cleanup, blocked-user stream
filtering, secure proxy headers, Varnish cache safety, unreblog and undo
federation repair, chained MRF policies, direct timelines, account deletion,
deactivated-user handling, WebFinger `application/ld+json`, Hubzilla quirks,
federated blocks, Undo Like support, and private/direct user timeline
visibility.

## Deep historical compatibility rows 1501-1600

These rows cover early dependency migrations, unfollow/block/unblock repair,
WebFinger XRD and HTTP signature deployment notes, password changes,
Mastodon/Twitter API relationship actions, CORS expose headers, list APIs,
tag de-duplication, locked account tracking, Follow Accept/Reject semantics,
list streaming, URL guessing hardening, custom emoji picker metadata,
controller error handling, and domain blocks.

| ---: | --- | --- | --- | --- |

## Deep historical compatibility rows 1601-1700

These rows cover OAuth Basic auth, OAuth error status codes, XML/WebFinger
hardening, upload de-duplication, ActivityPub quarantine, locked account and
follow-request behavior, `RejectNonPublic`, relationship policy switches,
invites, modern WebFinger JRD defaults, blocked-boost streaming, activity
lookup failures, and OAuth password-grant username/email compatibility.

rendering, media timeline streaming, reply filtering, Announce recipient
handling, object/activity normalization, `contentMap`, block relationship
cleanup, SimplePolicy accept lists, Mastodon API v2 search, EXIF stripping,
Article support, default scopes, automatic emoji shortcodes, status URL
rendering, NodeInfo media-proxy metadata, X.509 public-key output, message
summaries, and more robust object-to-actor association.

Legacy batch rows still awaiting strict confirmation: `0`

Important: `legacy-batch-reviewed` is advisory only. Any row after `15431` that still carries that prior state must be reviewed again in strict row order before it can count as complete.

Rows marked `todo` below must receive a durable commit-level disposition before the relevant strict cursor moves past them. Rows `13256` through `15431` now have strict historical dispositions again. Their previous pass state remains visible in the Prior state column as `historical-pass:*` provenance. Rows marked `base-history` before `13256` are still baseline unless a later subsystem audit reopens them.

## Disposition values

| Value | Meaning |
| --- | --- |
| `base-history` | At or before the current merge-base; inherited baseline context for merge-base accounting. Rows before `13256` are still eligible for the deep historical compatibility queue. |
| `todo` | Needs strict one-by-one review. |
| `present` | The upstream change is already present in Unfathomably. |
| `backported` | The upstream change was implemented or modernized in Unfathomably. |
| `superseded` | Unfathomably has a different implementation that covers the same need. |
| `not-applicable` | The change is CI/docs/frontend-bundle/release-only, reverted upstream, or otherwise intentionally not useful here. |
| `deferred` | Useful, but intentionally split out for a larger subsystem pass. |

## Deep historical compatibility rows 1801-1900

These rows cover AS2 responses for legacy notice/activity URLs, Plume Article
handling, actor extraction from unusual ActivityPub shapes, Mastodon suggestion
APIs, media descriptions and update endpoints, configurable instance/frontend
metadata, non-HTTP URI linking, signed ActivityPub Digest headers, duplicate
follow-request filtering, relay actors, virtual-service actor hiding, profile
emoji rendering, safe profile-name rendering, invite task compatibility, and
ActivityPub user emoji tags.

| Row | Commit | Date | Subject | Disposition |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |

## Deep historical compatibility rows 1901-2000

These rows cover Kroeg and Puckipedia ActivityPub compatibility, Mastodon app
response fields, redirect-following actor fetches, hashtag mention formatting,
sanitized Mastodon bios, Mastodon-style multiline bios, PeerTube video object
handling, missing/deleted-post notification safety, rejection of bogus ActivityPub
IDs, Mix task namespace cleanup, frontend collapse-subject configuration,
single-character mention matching, TLS SNI, attachment descriptions, email login,
orphaned-thread replies, and notification smoke coverage.

metadata, NodeInfo feature reporting, and SHA512-crypt/PBKDF2 authentication.

## Deep historical compatibility rows 4601-4700

Status: audited against the current Unfathomably backend and frontend trees.

Runtime source result:

- No new runtime source changes were required in this block. Current
  Unfathomably already carries or supersedes the applicable backend work, and
  the MastoFE/frontend-bundle rows do not apply to the Soapbox-derived FE.

Rows covered:

- `4601` to `4614`: invite token generation, TwitterAPI registration, admin
  invite endpoints, subscription docs, and deactivated-user OAuth token-exchange
  rejection. Current invite-token schema/tasks/admin controllers and OAuth
  controller already carry the useful work, including explicit disabled-account
  `403` responses with regression coverage.
- `4615` to `4625`: scheduled activities, `scheduled_at`, schedule worker,
  media attachment limits, `expires_at` naming, and subscription notification
  visibility. Current scheduled-activity controllers, views, Oban worker,
  migrations, and notification visibility checks already carry or supersede
  these rows.
- `4626` to `4649`: user-upgrade merge rows, emoji language tags, web-push job
  queueing, subscription cleanup on block, cached subscription resources,
  follower-count update on block, plaintext status rows, and `/api/pleroma/emoji`
  response-shape cleanup. Current Oban/user-refresh paths, language/content-map
  helpers, user subscription/block logic, follower counters, and emoji API
  already carry or supersede this work.
- `4650` to `4671`: undefined summary repair, email module renames,
  `notify_email`, direct-message conversations/participations, participation
  indexes, and OAuth consumer parameter wrapping. Current Transmogrifier summary
  handling, `Pleroma.Emails.*` modules, notification e-mail config, conversation
  and participation models, and OAuth controller already carry or supersede this
  work.
- `4672` to `4684`: MastoFE bundle rows, account deactivation cleanup,
  ActivityPub spec cleanup, OAuth template polish, Hackney `max_body` body
  retention, repeated-post `in_reply_to_id` rendering, and multiple notification
  destroy. Current deactivation flow, centralized Hackney `with_body` handling,
  status rendering, and notification controller already carry or supersede the
  runtime pieces.
- `4685` to `4700`: MastoFE download/install rows, SSL/password docs merges,
  formatter mention escaping revert/fallback, changelog rows, and user
  `email_notifications`. Current formatter tests, email notification storage,
  and source-install docs already carry or supersede the applicable rows.

## Deep historical compatibility rows 4401-4500

Status: audited against the current Unfathomably backend and frontend trees.

Runtime source result:

- Backported upstream row `4499` in modern form: `date_to_asctime/1` now accepts
  integer and float Unix timestamps as well as ISO 8601 strings. This preserves
  old OStatus/TwitterAPI compatibility for expiration and date values emitted as
  Unix time instead of silently producing an empty date.
- Added regression coverage for integer and float Unix timestamps while keeping
  invalid values on the safe empty-string path.
- No frontend source changes were required in this block.

Rows covered:

- `4401` to `4407`: missing Announce/object preload repair, notification id
  index, and default-avatar federation fixes. Current `associated_object_id/1`
  object joins, notification indexes, and ActivityPub actor image rendering
  already carry or supersede these rows.
- `4408` to `4424`: Mastodon-style OAuth errors, reply-count accounting,
  instance `registrations` and `languages`, ActivityRepresenter removal,
  ActivityPub actor image tests, app credential verification, and VAPID app
  responses. Current OAuth, instance rendering, app controller/view, and
  reply-count code already carry or supersede these rows.
- `4425` to `4439`: admin user search extraction, missing MastoAPI Announce
  timeline rows, OAuth consumer-account binding, admin user show, notification
  controls, and notification settings API. Current AdminAPI search/user
  controllers, notification settings, app/account OAuth support, and home
  timeline handling already carry or supersede these rows.
- `4440` to `4458`: pagination helper moves, atom-key cleanup, link-header
  cursor repair, documentation moves, reverted reply-count migration, and the
  old pre-Oban job queue. Current pagination/link-header helpers and Oban-based
  worker stack already carry or supersede these rows; documentation-only rows do
  not require runtime changes.
- `4459` to `4489`: fake status submission, BBS/SSH daemon experiment,
  default mail adapter config, fake-object scrubber behavior, emoji tag storage,
  force-login routing, and MastoFE auth routing. Current fake activity handling,
  scrubber cache behavior, emoji metadata, mail defaults, and frontend-auth
  routing already carry or supersede the useful parts; the BBS/SSH daemon rows
  remain intentionally not applicable.
- `4490` to `4500`: documentation cleanups, OStatus status-URL remote-follow
  handling, and Unix timestamp date handling. Current remote interaction already
  resolves status URLs through the object fetcher; Unix timestamp date handling
  is now backported.

Next row for the reopened historical audit: `4501` (`fd7dd8f9a`, 2019-04-01,
`Merge branch 'fix/default-mail-adapter' into 'develop'`).

## Deep historical compatibility rows 4301-4400

Status: audited against the current Unfathomably backend and frontend trees.

Runtime source result:

- Restored the legacy `/api/statusnet/config(.json)` controller action and
  documented response schema. The route already existed, but the current
  controller no longer implemented `config/2`, so older Pleroma-style clients
  could hit a dead compatibility endpoint.
- Backported the safe-DM compatibility signal from upstream row `4345`:
  `site.safeDMMentionsEnabled` is now returned as `"1"` or `"0"` in the legacy
  statusnet config response.
- Added regression coverage for the legacy statusnet config response and the
  safe-DM flag values.
- No frontend source changes were required in this block.

Rows covered:

- `4301` to `4318`: Twitter OAuth prototype merge rows, Pleroma-specific
  notification `is_seen` docs, Mastodon notification `exclude_types`, `limit`,
  and `min_id` support, external registration refactors, first-federation
  initial-post checks, account extension docs, and admin user ordering. Current
  Unfathomably already carries notification pagination/filter/read-state
  behaviour and external-registration support; the unfinished Twitter OAuth
  prototype rows are not taken.
- `4319` to `4328`: image-size optimization, upload-test asset updates, and
  ActivityPub public-addressing/followers-collection repair. Current
  Transmogrifier addressing normalizes malformed recipient lists, expands
  `as:Public` delivery to followers, and handles actors with missing follower
  collection data through the modern user/addressing helpers.
- `4329` to `4341`: admin user search improvements, Pleroma-FE bundle update,
  LDAP warning suppression, Ecto 3 migration timestamp fixes, and multiple OAuth
  consumer strategy support. Current code already has modern user search,
  current Ecto migration conventions, LDAP support, and OAuth consumer plumbing;
  frontend bundle rows do not apply to the Soapbox-derived FE.
- `4342` to `4362`: safe-DM mention option, util endpoint state, database-backed
  search ranking, Mastodon `conversation_id` extension, remote-report e-mail
  fixes, search `union_all`, NodeInfo feature flag, and null-content hotfix.
  Current code already carries safe-DM enforcement, NodeInfo/instance feature
  advertisement, search ranking work, conversation IDs, and null-content
  hardening; the missing legacy util config surface is now restored.
- `4363` to `4393`: doc deployment rows and the object-preload performance
  cluster. Current `Activity.with_preloaded_object/2`,
  `Activity.get_by_ap_id_with_object/1`, `Activity.create_by_id_with_object/1`,
  `Object.normalize/2` preloaded-object handling, timeline preloading,
  notification preloading, RichMedia Create-only crawling, and debug-level
  normalize warnings already carry or supersede these rows.
- `4394` to `4400`: eldap warning merge, KeywordPolicy nil-summary fix,
  DigitalOcean S3 `truncated_namespace`, non-public favourites, and S3 uploader
  merge rows. Current code already has nil-safe KeywordPolicy coverage,
  S3 `truncated_namespace`, and favourites visibility handling.

`fix up missing announcements with preloads`).

## Deep historical compatibility rows 4201-4300

Status: audited against the current Unfathomably backend and frontend trees.

Runtime source result:

- Backported and modernized the useful part of the upstream upload/media
  filename-hardening cluster around rows `4206` to `4208` and `4258` to `4262`.
  Reverse proxy attachment responses now parse quoted, unquoted, and RFC 5987
  `filename*` content-disposition filenames, then re-emit a sanitized quoted
  filename with embedded quotes, backslashes, and control characters escaped or
  neutralized.
- Added regression coverage for quoted filenames, unquoted filenames,
  `filename*`, and unsafe control characters.
- No frontend source changes were required in this block. Pleroma-FE/MastoFE
  bundle updates and login styling rows do not apply to the Soapbox-derived FE.

Rows covered:

- `4201` to `4208`: linting, CI Elixir 1.8.1 history, push activity-id merge,
  Mastodon screen-name extension merge, and upload filename/content-disposition
  hardening. CI/merge rows are not applicable; push activity IDs and screen-name
  compatibility are already present; media filename/header hardening is now
  backported in the current reverse-proxy path.
- `4209` to `4215`: Mastodon followers/following endpoints, user
  `delete_activities` task, OpenLDAP STARTTLS, and admin-user roles metadata.
  Current Unfathomably already provides Mastodon follower/following endpoints,
  LDAP STARTTLS support, and admin role/local/tag metadata; user cleanup is
  covered by the newer administration and database maintenance tooling.
- `4216` to `4230`: early Credo CI/readability cleanup and duplicate alias
  removal. These are style/CI rows and are superseded by current stricter
  release-engineering and compiler-warning cleanup work.
- `4231` to `4244`: admin account-view namespace cleanup, admin `local` and
  `tags` fields, CommonAPI unfollow/accept/reject follow-request unification,
  MastoFE content-type merge, and delete-activity federation fixes. Current
  admin view exposes local/roles/tags, follow transitions are CommonAPI-backed,
  frontend bundle rows do not apply, and local Delete activities federate
  through the modern pipeline.
- `4245` to `4257`: inbound `Flag` acceptance, flag-data normalization,
  forwarded reports, unified report e-mail, non-forwarded report recipient
  injection, notification `is_seen`, and extracted notification rendering.
  Current report/Flag/notification code already carries or supersedes this
  behavior, including the newer grouped-notification and report-forwarding work.
- `4258` to `4266`: media-proxy filename parsing improvements, attachment MIME
  type rendering, upload dedupe default history, and related docs/tests. Current
  status attachment rendering exposes MIME metadata and upload dedupe behavior
  is newer; reverse-proxy filename parsing/quoting is now hardened.
- `4267` to `4292`: Mastodon reblog hiding, streamer containment checks,
  filtering refactors, Twitter OAuth prototype rows, LDAP authorization tests,
  docs imports, notification single-read endpoint, OAuth `me` token response,
  and federated Flags merge. Reblog muting, streamer containment, LDAP tests,
  single-notification dismissal/read behavior, OAuth `me`, and federated reports
  are already present or superseded; unfinished Twitter OAuth prototype rows are
  not taken.
- `4293` to `4300`: admin user ordering, hashtag `rel` scrubber allowance,
  and Mastodon conversation API routing. Current admin user search/listing,
  scrubber policy, and conversation endpoints already carry or supersede these
  rows.

## Deep historical compatibility rows 4101-4200

Status: audited against the current Unfathomably backend tree.

Runtime source result:

- Backported the useful part of upstream row `4178` in modern form: the
  `Pleroma.HTTP.request/2` Tesla wrapper now converts adapter exceptions,
  throws, and exits into ordinary `{:error, reason}` tuples instead of letting
  HTTP adapter failures crash callers. This carries the original Pleroma
  hackney-safety intent while covering the current Gun, Finch, Hackney, and
  custom Tesla adapter paths.
- Added focused regression coverage for adapter exceptions and exits.
- No frontend source changes were required in this block.

Rows covered:

- `4101` to `4110`: private/direct mention timeline merge, note counter
  migration history, atomic note/follower counter updates, follow-request count
  removal, public-only status counts, follow-request SQL pagination, admin user
  API pagination, and rel=me bio metadata. Current Unfathomably already carries
  or supersedes these behaviours through query-based follow requests,
  public-only note accounting, cached relationship counters, admin pagination,
  and RelMe support.
- `4111` to `4117`: OpenLDAP/authenticator refactors, TODO removal, muted
  account stream filtering, and CommonAPI follow unification. Current code has
  the authenticator split, LDAP support, outgoing mute/reblog/domain filtering
  in `Streamer.filtered_by_user?/3`, and CommonAPI-centred follow paths.
- `4118` to `4134`: direct-message visibility for blocking users, delete
  activity audience expansion, TLS version history, MastoFE content type/default
  config history, local account disable flow, non-public Like addressing, status
  text in notifications, unused-code cleanup, and user search consolidation.
  Current code keeps Like followers-address leakage out of non-public objects,
  addresses Delete activities to the deleted object's original audience, uses
  modern TLS/client stack configuration, and has newer notification/search/user
  account behaviour.
- `4135` to `4148`: RichMedia bogus URI validation, parser helper tests,
  `local` to `only_local` naming history, NodeInfo staff metadata, CSP
  `connect-src` static URL handling, RelMe multi-value `rel` handling, and
  reserved-character upload URL escaping. Current code validates RichMedia URLs
  before fetching, exposes NodeInfo staff accounts, retains RelMe handling, and
  URI-encodes upload paths and link names.
- `4149` to `4163`: object-missing handling, MastoFE config history, web-push
  tests, Link header parameter preservation, object-check revert history, and
  final delete-audience adjustment. Current object fetch/delete/link-header
  paths already carry or supersede these behaviours.
- `4164` to `4184`: AntiFollowbot nil-display-name handling, frontend bundle
  updates, first-federation outbox fetch, admin status deletion, TLS 1.3 history,
  and HTTP adapter safety. Frontend bundle rows do not apply to the
  Soapbox-derived FE, first-federation fetch/admin-delete behaviour is already
  covered by newer code, and the HTTP adapter safety row is now backported in
  adapter-neutral form.
- `4185` to `4200`: client documentation updates, Mastodon streaming delete
  payload compatibility, Mastodon API locality indicator, login-page styling,
  differences documentation, account fetch by nickname, Twitter sign-in WIP, and
  web-push activity IDs. Current streaming emits the deleted activity id,
  statuses include `pleroma.local`, account rendering has nickname fallback
  coverage, and push payloads include `pleroma.activity_id`; documentation,
  frontend bundle, and unfinished Twitter sign-in rows do not apply.

Next row for the reopened historical audit: `4201` (`07277879e`, 2019-03-11,
`Linting.`).

- This block covered RichMedia relative-path and card URI hardening,
  reachability update optimizations, Mogrify documentation/deprecation
  guidance, the finalized hide-network split, Hellthread delist improvements,
  RIFF MIME detection, user tag/untag tasks, MRF TagPolicy, ActivityPub C2S
  `/api/ap/whoami`, bare tag normalization, nil-content HTML handling,
  RichMedia card rendering without images, RichMedia JSON-encoding validation,
  admin/moderator metadata and role visibility, ExSyslogger examples, OAuth2
  redirect URI and `Mastodon-Local` app hardening, and MRF KeywordPolicy.
- Current Unfathomably BE already carries these fixes directly, carries later
  broader implementations, or has moved the historical behavior into modern
  RichMedia card/parser/backfill code, instance reachability tracking,
  upload/Mogrify configuration, hide-followers/hide-follows fields,
  HellthreadPolicy, MIME/upload handling, admin user tagging, TagPolicy,
  ActivityPub C2S routes, Transmogrifier tag normalization, HTML nil guards,
  account/admin metadata rendering, OAuth app/redirect validation, and
  KeywordPolicy configuration.
- No live deployment was required for this block because this pass changed
  only audit and changelog documentation.

## Deep historical compatibility rows 3501-3600

Status: audited, no runtime patch required in this pass.

Summary:

- This block covered Flake ID migration cleanup, the old frontend configuration
  mechanism, unreachable federation target retirement, reachability-aware
  publishing and incoming federation updates, AntiFollowbot MRF, Flake worker ID
  hardening, ActivityPub activity visibility SQL fixes, attention-list logic,
  Mastodon-style direct-message addressing fixes, nil-safe remote content
  limits, multi-hashtag timelines, Mastodon status cards/RichMedia support,
  RichMedia card provider names, first external URL extraction, preload joins,
  web-push endpoint length, nil URI RichMedia hardening, account admin/moderator
  metadata, favourites index support, safe rendering, invalid likes cleanup,
  `/notice/:id` frontend fallback, bookmarks, embedded status cards, RichMedia
  parser rescue behavior, removal of the old RichMedia parse endpoint, RichMedia
  backfill/cache priming, and media-proxied card images.
- Current Unfathomably BE already carries these fixes directly, carries later
  broader implementations, or has moved the historical behavior into modern
  Flake ID compatibility, instance reachability and dormancy tracking,
  federation publisher handling, MRF policy modules, ActivityPub visibility
  migrations, direct-message visibility helpers, hashtag timeline code,
  RichMedia parser/backfill/card rendering, Mastodon-compatible account/status
  views, safe render helpers, bookmark/bookmark-folder APIs, and frontend
  fallback routing.
- No live deployment was required for this block because this pass changed
  only audit and changelog documentation.

Next row for the reopened historical audit: `3601` (`9560abea1`, 2019-01-28,
`[#534] Refactoring / tweaks per MR review.`).

  OpenGraph/RichMedia parsing and caching, ActivityPub C2S outbox splitting and
  Delete handling, Phoenix-style websocket configuration, reserved `/media`
  routing, follow-request safety for inactive accounts, image descriptions,
  ActivityPub tombstone rendering safety, emoji-safe stripped summaries,
  duplicate follow-notification prevention, syslog support, search visibility
  checks, pinned posts, signup autofollow, local/remote user predicate cleanup,
  activity+json object responses, and the old TwitterAPI pinned-status surface.

  broader implementations, or has moved the historical behavior into modern
  RichMedia, Oban/federation, ActivityPub C2S, account visibility, pinned
  object, media-proxy, syslog, and Mastodon-compatible API code.

  only audit and changelog documentation.

## Deep historical compatibility rows 2601-2700

Status: audited, no runtime patch required in this pass.

Summary:

- This block covered user-info transition fixes, locked and deactivated account safety, streamer/list privacy, unauthenticated public websocket compatibility, CSP/security header adjustments, MRF user allowlist support, HTTP signature/WebFinger hardening, reverse proxy/upload handling, OGG/Theora MIME handling, and early admin/API cleanup.
- Current Unfathomably BE already carries these fixes directly, carries a later and broader implementation, or no longer has the historical code path that needed the fix.
- No live deployment was required for this block because this pass changed only audit and changelog documentation.

## Deep historical compatibility rows 2501-2600

These rows cover the November 2018 upstream work around HTTP security sample
configuration, nil display-name fallback, secure cookie names, media-proxy CSP
and filenames, direct-message pagination, user search, the historical MDII
uploader, ActivityPub spoofing/origin-containment hardening, early AdminAPI and
relay task behavior, secure deletes, AS2 notice rendering, object-view privacy,
and early user count/deactivation fixes. The current Unfathomably tree was
checked for the runtime behavior; no runtime patch was needed for this block.

| Row | Commit | Date | Subject | Disposition |
| ---: | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| 2501 | `2829fa418` | 2018-11-12 | sample config: chase http_security change | Present, superseded. Current `:http_security` defaults and config descriptions use the maintained HTTPSecurity naming. |
| 2502 | `54923c2e5` | 2018-11-12 | Merge branch 'feature/csp-plug' into 'develop' | Not applicable. Merge-only; HTTPSecurity behavior is covered in the surrounding rows. |
| 2503 | `cb6fd7386` | 2018-11-12 | Twitter API: Fall back to user.nickname if user has no name | Present. Current account/admin/event rendering falls back from `user.name` to `user.nickname`. |
| 2504 | `35895b1c4` | 2018-11-13 | Add Whalebird as a client application in README | Superseded. Historical README client mention is not runtime-applicable to Unfathomably. |
| 2505 | `db78c7286` | 2018-11-12 | Twitter API: Add tests for nil names. | Present, superseded. Modern account rendering tests and fallbacks cover nil display names. |
| 2506 | `0ce562313` | 2018-11-12 | Merge branch 'twitter-api-null-display-name' into 'develop' | Not applicable. Merge-only; behavior covered at row 2503. |

## Deep historical compatibility rows 2401-2500

These rows cover the November 2018 upstream work around runtime configuration,
OAuth/login cleanup, notification read state, JSON-LD/LitePub contexts,
mention extraction, OStatus filtering, internal ActivityPub data stripping,
CORS, HTTP security headers, and list streaming. They are pre-Rebased common
history commits, but this block was checked against the current
Unfathomably source so fork-era changes are not assumed to preserve the old
behavior automatically.

| Row | Commit | Date | Subject | Disposition |
| ---: | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| 2401 | `6fe23c545` | 2018-11-05 | Runtime configured router | Present, superseded. Current router and endpoint configuration is runtime-aware through `Pleroma.Config`, release/runtime providers, and ConfigDB transfer support. |
| 2402 | `fd0e7d18d` | 2018-11-05 | handle_continue is OTP21+ | Not applicable. Historical OTP compatibility adjustment; current supported OTP/Elixir stack already uses `handle_continue/2` where appropriate. |
| 2403 | `03702e4c4` | 2018-11-05 | Merge branch 'runtime-emojis' into 'develop' | Not applicable. Merge-only; runtime emoji behavior is covered by the concrete rows in this cluster. |
| 2404 | `83911b144` | 2018-11-05 | Fix wrong return when finmoji is disabled | Present, superseded. Finmoji/static fallback paths are handled by current static route and emoji loader behavior. |
| 2405 | `0f75ed33f` | 2018-11-06 | Merge branch 'runtime-emojis-fix-finmoji-return' into 'develop' | Not applicable. Merge-only; behavior covered at row 2404. |

## Deep historical compatibility rows 2301-2400

Reviewed on 2026-07-05 as part of the reopened full upstream-Pleroma audit.
This block covers late-2018 hardening around safe rendering, recipient handling,
HTTP pooling, media proxy leakage, upload limits, cache invalidation, Page
objects, CORS, hashtag search, and runtime emoji loading. No runtime source
patch was required in this pass: current Unfathomably either contains the old
fix directly, carries a stronger modern descendant, or has made the old
frontend/documentation-only change obsolete.

modern descendants, or has replaced the old frontend/doc-only change with newer
project-specific release material.

Next row for the reopened historical audit: `2301` (`b386888a0`, 2018-10-25,
`Pleroma.Web.MastodonAPI.MastodonAPIController: fallback for try_render/4`).

## Deep historical compatibility rows 2101-2200

These rows cover the 2018 authentication plug split, legacy-password upgrade,
disabled-user protection, relationship response compatibility, suggestion limits,
OAuth redirect encoding, configurable HTML scrubbers, SimplePolicy refactors,
bot profile updates, status emoji picker metadata, inline-image media-proxy
scrubbing, OPTIONS responses, attachment fallback rendering, reply counts,
endorsement stubs, dynamic remote-profile refresh, user-selectable rich-text
scrub policy, push subscription CRUD, S3 key sanitization, and Kroeg endpoint
hardening.

| 2436 | `144dc048b` | 2018-11-08 | Only consider `to` recipients as mention targets | Backported and strengthened. Current notification logic already ignores cc-only recipients, and outgoing generated `Mention` tags now use `to` recipients only while preserving group-aware suppression. |
| 2437 | `3e33479c0` | 2018-11-08 | Generate mention tags from `to` users only | Backported and modernized by row 2436. |

| 2450 | `b3c360ce2` | 2018-11-09 | Add fallback notification receiver lookup | Backported and modernized. The fallback now returns `{[], []}` to match the modern enabled/disabled receiver tuple contract, with regression coverage. |

| 2452 | `e4971553c` | 2018-11-09 | Share object-type list for mention extraction | Implemented/superseded by current object validation, notification, and group-aware ActivityPub handling. |
